item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Heartwarming zombie love <3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
FWIW, it was biologically interesting.

Ethically almost certainly on the bad side (dunno if it's possible to get genuinely voluntership from either the tribespeople or especially the D-Classes), but interesting. Tone didn't bode too well considering it was the only real test done there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This bothered me as well, but the quoted "scripture" does explain that Tlaloc's priests were cast out post-curse because… well, I'd rather not live next door to a bunch of unrotting skeleton monsters, either. Can't blame 'em. So that might explain why we're seeing Aztec deities/metaphysical concepts where we normally wouldn't expect to see them.

Still… the Aztec gods being referenced here still seem wrong somehow. And the actual quoted "artifact" explains things a bit top nicely and comes off rather narmy. That's the real reason for my downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Had to downvote this SCP due to the odd usage of prehispanic gods and poor research on the subject.

First of all, the Yucatan peninsula was inhabited by mayans, not by mexicas (aztecs) so it doesn't make sense to use mexica's gods in a geographic area occupied by a completely different civilization.

Second, Tlaloc (aztec god) is the water cycle (not the god of xxxxx), and its mayan counterpart is Chaac.

Third, Tlaloc is not the god of fertility, that's the Coatlicue for the Aztecs and Ixchel for the Mayans.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Self-edited a low-quality, kind of spammy post

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Just to put these comments to rest, "Jewelled Turkey" is indeed another moniker for Chalchiuhtotolin, which/who was indeed the Aztec god of pestilence. I did my research for this.

E: Hmm, I seem to have downvoted my own page somehow. It totally wasn't in an effort to dip below +40 for that contest or anything, honest!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like this.
+1

Also

Such reverence for Tlaloc displeased the Jeweled Turkey, Chalchiuhtotolin.

Pfft, Jeweled Turkey.
And this makes me wonder, did the people of this region and era know about Turkeys? I assume they were native to North America only.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: At least it is not supposed to be "Great Turkey".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, if you really wanted to see the part I deleted, it'll still be in the page's history that you can find at the bottom of the page. That said, you may see why people generally had an issue with it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I liked this. The only thing that confused me was the reproductive cycle, but according to the comments you deleted a part about that?

Also, I felt it was kinda weird that these guys knew exactly when they were going to die based on the re-composition of their bodies, but I guess their skin could fully manifest before their organs so they'd have no idea when death was actually coming unless they opened themselves up (which makes me wonder if they can be injured).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As is Jewelled. I believe it's deliberate.

I like this one

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is the evil god supposed to be Jade Turkey or Jaded Turkey? As of right now both are used in the translation

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well done with the changes, changed to an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you for the encouragement, to you and everyone else. It'll help motivate me with further writing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is pretty awesome, all things considered.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm honestly going to say that I thought it was nice for atmosphere, the way the Foundation "breeds" D-Class, but I wasn't particularly attached to it either way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agreed with that. Because "Let's see what happens!" is probably the 2nd worst reason to do something and for the various reasons of probably half a dozen others, I decided that my article would fare best without it. It didn't add much, and clearly rubbed people the wrong way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: To be fair if there is a justification for it or something I have very little qualms. It just seemed there just to be there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Already thirded? The feedback during drafting was pretty 50-50, but I suppose the majority rules, now.

Edit: Removed by popular request.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thirded. Rape-as-research is off-putting in a very undesirable way, the tone of the scientist just makes it worse.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, seconding this. The rest I liked, that part just seemed really off, and not in a good way. Do you really want to be reminding people of The Saddest Necrophile?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't get the point of presumably forcing people to be impregnated by corpses and to impregnate corpses. It's a tad skeevy. No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You FINALLY posted it, huh. NOW, I can upvote it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No problem.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fixed the problem. Thanks for the notice.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There are a lot of XXXX left. I'd fix them per te, but you ain't letting me.
